ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR FURTHER RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORY 17.1; AND DENYING PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR AND ORDER THAT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS BE DEEMD ADIMITTED; FOR SANCTIONS AND FEES. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF ALLAN SCHUMAN'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR FURTHER RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORY 17.1 AND FOR AN ORDER THAT REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS BE DEEMED ADMITTED IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S VERIFIED RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE, WAS SERVED PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION. ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND FOR ADMISSIONS TO BE DEEMED ADMITTED IS DENIED BUT MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $840.00 IS AWARDED AGAINST THE ATTORNEY FOR THE DEFENDANT, TO BE PAID WITHIN TWENTY DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. SEE CCP 2033.280 (C). THE MOTION TO COMPEL A RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 IS GRANTED. THE VERIFIED RESPONSE SHALL BE SERVED WITHIN SEVEN COURT DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtns(S) For Order For Further Response To Interrogatory 17.1 And For An Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Wayne Lesser, Esq. (415-457-7585) for Allan Schuman Plaintiff and moving party
David Golden, Esq. of Golden Kopcke specially appearing for Warren Edward, Defendant opposing party.

ARGUED. All Parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem hearing the matter. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: PLAINTIFF ALLAN SCHUMAN's Motion For Order For Further Response To Interrogatory 17.1 And For An Order That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted is granted and denied as follows: Defendant's verified responses to plaintiffs Request for Admissions, Set one, was served prior to the hearing on this motion. Accordingly, the motion to compel and for Admissions to be deemed admitted is denied but monetary sanctions of $840.00 is awarded against the attorney for the defendant, to be paid within twenty days of the date of this order. See CCP 2033.280 (c). The motion to compel a response to Form Interrogatory 17.1 is granted. The verified response shall be served within seven court days of the date of this order. Order is signed in open Court. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File X-Complt
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wayne Lewis Lesser, Esq., 415.563.2111, for plaintiff Allan Schuman; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ALLAN SCHUMAN's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Allan M. Schuman may file its proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sara Lerschen CSR6213.
